rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GERALD A. DURKIN AND ELSIE J. DURKIN 2015 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Sell The Trust Property "As-Is"
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition does not appear to have been signed and verified by the petitioner. [Probate Code Section 1020.] (2) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (3) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (4) Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. If a party entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b)]. (5) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (6) The register of actions still does not reflect an order dispensing with notice to Charles Lucero and nothing has been filed to indicate that his interest in the trust has been satisfied. (7) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Deadline is 11/27/23 for providing the assigned examiner the proposed order and courtesy copies. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A petition should include a PRAYER requesting relief, not a conclusion.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Statement Of Interest And Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interests In Estate And Authorizing Distribution Directly To Celtic Research
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 06/12/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Direct notice must be given to the personal representative. See CRC 7.51(a). 4) Direct notice must be given to Bridge Financial Funding LLC as a person who requested special notice, in addition to a separate copy given to its counsel. 5) Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must be served on all persons who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND BESSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Receipt and Acknowledgement of Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. (2) The Proof of Service -- Civil filed on 10/2/23 is not of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures attached to the Notice of [Amended] Petition to Administer Estate (not attached to a petition). (4) No proof of publication of the amended petition has been filed. (5) The caption of the amended petition is incomplete, if the petitioner is seeking full IAEA authority. (6) Item 2d of the amended petition is incomplete. (7) Item 3d of the amended petition is incomplete. (8) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the amended petition must be answered. (9) How does a police report support the petition? Was the original will in her bag that was stolen from her car, not in the decedent's possession? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If there are no assets in the estate [item 3d of the pleading], why is a formal estate necessary? Item 7 of a petition for probate should not be answered when issue survived.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 2/27/23 have not yet been addressed. 1) The I&A showed just $155,000 representing "Final Cash Distribution from Estate of Frank Chew." The order for final distribution in case PES-19-302573 ordered $283,785.53 net cash (as of 11/30/19) to be distributed to this executor, who was also the administrator of Frank Chew's estate. 2) The verification of the petition is incomplete (incomplete date). 3) The proof of service filed on 12/7/22 shows that notice was mailed to Ming Yu Wu as the trustee of the Nancy Ngai Ying Chew Trust and to James Chew as "Beneficiary," but a review of earlier papers shows that the executor, when petitioning for appointment, claimed that James Chew (son), Yan Miao Wu (niece), and Ming Yu Wu (nephew/Executor) were all successor trustees of the Nancy Ngai Ying Chew Trust. The executor must clarify who the trustee of the devisee trust is and why James Chew is no longer a trustee. 4) Also, the face of the Notice of Hearing shows that this is a hearing of the "First and Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account," etc., when the personal representative is an executor. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHELE PAYNE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Administrator To Establish Estate'S Claim Of Ownership To Royalty Interests And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate And Heirs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: 1) No proposed order submitted. LRSF 14.7.A 2) Crosspointe 2, LLC (and Teichert if applicable) must be served at least 30 days before the hearing in the manner required by Probate Code Sec.851(a)(2) & CCP Sec.413.10 et seq. 3) Item 3 of the DE-115 form must provide a sufficient description of the subject property ("LLC" is not the subject property - royalty interest, right to receive royalties, and/or other contractual rights arising under Crosspointe's agreement with Crosspointe 2 are). PC Sec.851(c). 4) Petition seeks to include whom Crosspointe 2, LLC needs to make payment when the estate is closed; however, such order seems more appropriate in order approving final distribution. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHARINE PENSON MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner is requesting reimbursement of $48,141 for attorney's fees that she has already paid for, including $17,970 that was paid to the current attorney (at $600/hr) and for services performed prior to probate administration, which he alleges were necessary to determine whether probate was needed. 2) Attorney requests attorney fees of $112,662 incurred through 3/31/23 and states that they will cap fees at 4%. Petitioner alleges that compensations should be fixed at 4% pursuant to terms of the will, but the will states that the 4% should be given to the executrix, not the attorney. The 4% also seems to be a maximum allowed and seems to apply to all legal expenses incurred, including costs, and it is not known exactly how much has been paid total in legal expenses, including in Mexico. 3) Petitioner alleges that she is allowed a compensation of 4% of the value of the estate; however, the will states that the 4% is left for the purposes that are indicated in the will. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTH JOHNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/27/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/21/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 09/18/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition caption includes settlement of a final account (in addition to a final report), but no accounting schedules were attached. If the petitioner is requesting approval of a final report on waiver of account, the petition caption must be amended and renoticed. 4) The account statement shows the petitioner's title as trustee of the devisee trust. Has the petitioner distributed all the assets to the devisee trust without prior court approval? 5) Each asset on hand must be specifically described. CRC 7.651. A specific amount of cash on hand as of a date certain must be alleged. The name and number of shares of each security as of a date certain must be alleged. If the number of shares is different from the number listed on the inventory and appraisal, the difference must be explained, even if the account is waived. CRC 7.550(b)(2) & (3). 6) WBD shares are not listed on the inventory and appraisal. How did the petitioner acquire those shares? 7) A detailed schedule of losses must be filed in support of the statutory fee base. CRC 7.550(b)(6). 8) The cash on hand appears insufficient to pay the statutory fees and reserve. The securities must be partially liquidated to increase the estate cash on hand. An appearance may be required on 11/27/2023, if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALZINA MARIE RAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Distribute Share Of Estate Of Heir Marcus Mcclure To Special Needs Trust For His Benefit
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/2023, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 08/21/2023 & 09/18/2023 hearings) remain unaddresed: If there is no conservatorship case, a petition to establish a special needs trust must be filed in a new trust case which must be opened under the name of the proposed trust. The following issues must also be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed trust must be attached to the order. LRSF 14.48(B). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Please provide a specific description and value of each asset distributable to Marcus McClure from this estate, together with annual income from each asset, for the purpose of setting the trustee's initial bond. 4) No guardian ad litem has been appointed for Marcus McClure. Does he qualify as a person who lacks legal capacity to make decisions within the meaning of Probate Code section 1003(a)(2)? 5) A budget of anticipated expenditures to be paid out of the proposed trust must be provided. LRSF 14.49(B). 6) Please identify all public benefits received by Marcus McClure. 7) Please identify issue of Marcus McClure. If he has no issue, please identify his heirs. 8) Does the petitioner, if appointed as trustee, intend to carry on the day-to-day activity of the trust in the county where the petitioner resides? If so, should the Court transfer jurisdiction to the county of the petitioner's residence per section 3604(a)(1)? If no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues, an appearance may be required on 11/22/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA GRIFFIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared: (1) Unless Petition #8 is supplemented to set forth heirs at law, need a declaration of due diligent search of the decedent's heirs at law. Petition merely states that decedent was not survived by no blood relatives. (2) Duties and Liabilities [Form DE 147] not on file. (3) Confidential Statement of Driver's License and Birth Date [Form DE-147S not on file. LRSF 14.18.E. (4) Proposed Order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. (5) If surviving spouse is the only successor in interest, if administration is needed. (6) Notice of Petition to Administer to William Brotherton. Notice of instruction of remote appearance to be served as well. (7) Notice of Petition to Administer with a copy of petition to Scott J. Judson who filed RSN. Notice of instruction of remote appearance to be served as well. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
